seoTitle: Print the General Ledger in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to find, customize, and print your full General Ledger report in QuickBooks Online for audit-ready paper or digital records.
intro: Your General Ledger is the master record of every business transaction, and QuickBooks Online can compile it into a scannable, print-ready document without you having to manually piece it together. This guide covers finding the report, setting the right date range and layout, and printing or saving it as a PDF.

steps.heading: Open the Reports section
steps.body: From the sidebar, hover over Reports and click through to the report library.

steps.heading: Search for General Ledger
steps.body: Type General Ledger into the search bar and select it from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Set your date range and method
steps.body: Use the customization toolbar to set your financial date range and choose between Cash and Accrual accounting.

steps.heading: Run and customize columns
steps.body: Click Run Report, then use the Gear icon in the top right to adjust which columns are visible.

steps.heading: Print or save as PDF
steps.body: Click Export/Print, choose Print or Save as PDF, switch your printer to landscape orientation for full column visibility, and review the print preview before printing.

faq.question: Why should I use landscape orientation for this report?
faq.answer: The General Ledger has many columns, and landscape orientation helps make sure they all fit on the page without getting cut off.

faq.question: Can I filter the ledger to just one account?
faq.answer: The report customization options let you adjust which columns and details show, which can help narrow the focus of what you're reviewing.

faq.question: What's the difference between viewing this in Cash versus Accrual?
faq.answer: Cash basis reflects when money actually moved, while Accrual reflects when transactions were recorded, so toggling changes which entries appear.

faq.question: Should I keep a digital copy in addition to a paper printout?
faq.answer: Yes, saving a PDF backup alongside any physical printouts is a good practice for preserving historical data.
